summary: FPV drones and loitering munitions continue to play a central role in frontline operations, with the "Rubicon" Center reporting over 24,000 enemy targets destroyed, including 36.7% unmanned systems and 36.4% in the previous count. Specific incidents include the destruction of a Russian TOS-1A MLRS by a Bulava loitering munition followed by FPV drones, and the capture of a Ukrainian "Shark-M" UAV after its parachute deployed during a kinetic intercept by the "Yolka" air defense drone. Ukrainian forces used FPV drones to repel Russian motorcycle assaults in Zaporizhzhia and struck Russian soldiers attempting to assault positions on motorcycles. Russian soldiers were also killed by FPV drones after feigning surrender. Ukrainian EW interference was observed, with operators attempting to force FPV drones to land. In Kharkiv, night FPV drones from the KVN team destroyed a Russian Bogdan self-propelled artillery unit after exposing its cover.
